vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1201: Hybrid context distribution: deprecate devroot, layered global/project/workspace, multi-harness sync

plan.title: Hybrid context distribution: deprecate devroot, layered global/project/workspace, multi-harness sync
plan.author: agent:gpt-5.5

narratives.Problem: PAN-1201 was reopened because two original user-visible contracts remain unmet: the plural `pan projects add <path>` registration command is absent, and the dashboard has no Context tab/page that edits the global/project/workspace context layers with Claude/Pi previews and an injected prompt audit. The branch already contains the layered context CLI/sync implementation, workspace context assembly, Pi loading, and sync-sources consolidation, so the remaining plan must finish the surfaced gaps without regressing those implemented contracts.
narratives.Proposal: Add a plural `pan projects` command group as the preferred alias over the existing singular project group. Then add typed dashboard context APIs, async server routes, a Monaco-backed Context page, live per-harness previews, explicit Save & Sync behavior, navigation integration, tests, docs, and isolated browser UAT. Preserve `.pan/context` as the implemented project/workspace path convention and keep server routes free of synchronous filesystem/process calls.
narratives.Constraint: Do not rewrite already-shipped PAN-1201 subsystems unless a regression test fails. Dashboard server code must use async filesystem/process APIs and path allowlists. Save/sync operations are explicit user actions only. Existing singular `pan project` remains supported for backwards compatibility.
narratives.Risk: The highest-risk bead is the dashboard context route because it writes user/project context files and could become an arbitrary file write if path validation is wrong. The second risk is frontend bundle/test churn from Monaco. The plan isolates these behind contracts, route tests, and a mockable editor wrapper.
narratives.Alternative: A smaller textarea-only dashboard page was considered, but the PRD explicitly calls for Monaco. A frontend-only preview renderer was also considered, but a server preview route keeps the frontend from duplicating Panopticon context rendering semantics.

autoDecisions.summary: Scope this reopened plan to the two unmet issue-comment surfaces plus regression tests/docs rather than redoing the existing layered sync implementation.
autoDecisions.rationale: The latest issue comment is authoritative about why PAN-1201 was reopened, and discovery found current code/tests/docs for most original acceptance criteria. Replanning the whole feature would create unnecessary churn and increase risk of content loss.

autoDecisions.summary: Preserve `.pan/context` for project/workspace layers despite older PRD text mentioning `.panopticon/context`.
autoDecisions.rationale: Current implementation comments and project rules follow PAN-967's `.pan/` lifecycle convention. Splitting dashboard work onto `.panopticon/` would create two context systems and regress existing tests.

autoDecisions.summary: Use a debounced server preview API instead of duplicating the templating engine in the browser.
autoDecisions.rationale: The existing renderer lives in Node-side code and can stay authoritative; live preview still feels live with debounce while preserving one set of semantics for Claude/Pi harness blocks.
No code editor dependency currently exists, and the PRD specifically requests Monaco. The wrapper can be mocked in tests to avoid brittle worker setup.

items.title: PAN-1201: Add plural `pan projects` command group

narrative.Action: Modify `src/cli/index.ts` around the existing `const project = program.command('project')` registration. Add a sibling plural command group `program.command('projects')` that registers `add`, `list`, `show`, `remove`, and `init` with the same options and action handlers (`projectAddCommand`, `projectListCommand`, `projectShowCommand`, `projectRemoveCommand`, `projectInitCommand`). Keep the singular group working unchanged for backwards compatibility. Add or update a CLI command test/fixture so `pan projects add <path>` is recognized and dispatches to the same handler shape as `pan project add <path>`. Do not change project registration semantics in `src/cli/commands/project.ts` in this bead.

subItems.title: `pan projects add <path>` is registered with the same options as `pan project add <path>`: `--name`, `--type`, `--linear-team`, and `--rally-project`.
The existing singular `pan project add <path>` command still works and is not removed or renamed.
CLI help/test fixtures expose the plural `projects` command so the issue's documented registration surface is discoverable.

items.title: PAN-1201: Prefer plural project command in copy and docs

narrative.Action: Update user-facing text that instructs users how to register projects so it uses `pan projects ...` as the preferred command. Touch known references from discovery: `src/cli/commands/project.ts` messages at the existing `pan project remove/list/add` guidance, `src/cli/commands/context-layers.ts` migration hint, `src/cli/commands/test.ts` project registration hint, `docs/CONTEXT-LAYERS.md`, and the relevant generated/help fixture if needed. Preserve mention of singular `pan project` only where documenting backwards compatibility.

subItems.title: New or changed user-facing registration guidance says `pan projects add <path>` rather than `pan project add <path>`.
Migration output in `pan context migrate` suggests the plural command for skipped non-interactive registration.
Docs explain that singular `pan project` remains a compatibility alias if mentioned at all.

items.title: PAN-1201: Define dashboard context API contracts

narrative.Action: Add typed context API shapes in `packages/contracts/src/context-layers.ts` (or an equivalent clearly named contracts module) and export them from `packages/contracts/src/index.ts`. Include types/schemas for `ContextLayerKind` (`global`, `project`, `workspace`), registered project summaries, workspace summaries, editable layer records (`kind`, `file`, `exists`, `content`, `editable`, optional `projectKey`/`workspacePath`), preview request/response, save request/response, and sync response. Name harness preview fields explicitly for `claude-code`, `pi`, and `fullPrompt`. Keep these contracts UI-agnostic and aligned with existing `Harness` values from `packages/contracts/src/types.ts`. Add package-level unit tests only if this package has nearby schema/type tests; otherwise ensure the root typecheck exercises the exports.

subItems.title: Contracts represent all three editable layers and both v1 harness previews without frontend/server-only naming drift.
The contracts distinguish preview-only operations from save/sync operations so the frontend cannot accidentally write during live preview.
`npm run typecheck` can import the new context contracts from `@panctl/contracts` in both dashboard server and frontend code.

items.title: PAN-1201: Add async dashboard context layer routes

narrative.Action: Create `src/dashboard/server/routes/context.ts` and register `contextRouteLayer` in `src/dashboard/server/server.ts`. Implement async, allowlisted endpoints for the dashboard page: `GET /api/context/layers` returns registered projects from the async `listProjects()` Effect API, workspace candidates under each project's configured workspaces directory, current global/project/workspace layer file paths, existence flags, and content read with `fs/promises`; `POST /api/context/preview` accepts draft layer content and returns rendered `claude-code`, `pi`, and `fullPrompt` previews using pure `renderForHarness` plus explicit Panopticon-injected prompt sections; `PUT /api/context/layers` writes only the selected canonical layer file after resolving it from the allowlist. Do not import `src/lib/sync.ts` or call `renderGlobalLayer`/`renderProjectLayer` in this route because those paths use sync FS. Add route/helper tests under `tests/dashboard/context-routes.test.ts` (or nearby route test location) with temporary PANOPTICON_HOME/project/workspace fixtures.

subItems.title: Route handlers use `fs/promises`/Effect async project APIs and contain no `readFileSync`, `writeFileSync`, `readdirSync`, `statSync`, or `execSync` in dashboard-reachable code.

Saving a global, project, or workspace layer writes only `~/.panopticon/context/global.md`, `<project>/.pan/context/project.md`, or `<workspace>/.pan/context/workspace.md` selected through allowlisted registry/workspace data.
Previewing draft content renders `{{#harness:claude}}` and `{{#harness:pi}}` blocks consistently with `src/lib/context-layers/harness.ts` and does not write files.
The `fullPrompt` preview clearly shows the complete Panopticon-injected bundle Panopticon controls and labels any unavailable private harness base prompt as unavailable rather than fabricating it.

items.title: PAN-1201: Add explicit dashboard Save & Sync endpoint

narrative.Action: In `src/dashboard/server/routes/context.ts`, add `POST /api/context/sync` that runs `pan context sync` only when the user explicitly clicks Save & Sync. Use async child process execution (`execFile` promisified or `spawn` with Promise wrapping), return stdout/stderr/status in the typed response, and never use `execSync`. Add tests that mock the async runner and verify the route is not called by preview/save endpoints.

subItems.title: `POST /api/context/sync` runs `pan context sync` asynchronously and reports success/failure without blocking the dashboard event loop.
Live preview and ordinary save requests do not invoke sync; sync occurs only through the explicit sync endpoint.
Errors from `pan context sync` surface as a structured error response the frontend can display in a toast.

items.title: PAN-1201: Add frontend context API client and hooks

narrative.Action: Create `src/dashboard/frontend/src/lib/contextApi.ts` (or colocate in the new context page directory) with typed fetch helpers for `GET /api/context/layers`, `POST /api/context/preview`, `PUT /api/context/layers`, and `POST /api/context/sync`. Use the contracts from `@panctl/contracts`. Add React Query hooks or plain helpers used by the page; keep path/query construction in one place. Tests should mock `fetch` and assert request methods/bodies for load, preview, save, and sync.

subItems.title: Frontend API helpers use the shared contract types and do not hardcode divergent layer/harness string shapes.
Preview helper sends draft content to `POST /api/context/preview`; save helper sends writes only to `PUT /api/context/layers`; sync helper calls only `POST /api/context/sync`.
API helper tests cover non-OK responses so ContextPage can display meaningful errors.

items.title: PAN-1201: Build Context page editor shell

narrative.Action: Add `@monaco-editor/react` and `monaco-editor` using Bun, then create `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/context/ContextPage.tsx` and a small `ContextEditor.tsx` wrapper around Monaco. The page layout should have a left rail with layer selector (Global, Project, Workspace), project/workspace selectors populated from the API, file path/existence labels, a center Monaco markdown editor, and loading/error/empty states. For workspace layer, explain that `.pan/context/workspace.md` is auto-assembled and may not exist until a workspace is created. Mock the editor wrapper in tests to avoid Monaco worker brittleness.

subItems.title: The page can load and edit global, selected project, and selected workspace layer content from the context API.
The center editor uses Monaco through a wrapper component and falls back cleanly in tests via a mock, not through duplicated page logic.
The UI labels project/workspace paths as `.pan/context/project.md` and `.pan/context/workspace.md`, matching the current implementation.

items.title: PAN-1201: Add live previews and Save & Sync behavior

narrative.Action: Extend `ContextPage.tsx` with the right preview rail and footer actions. Debounce editor changes and call `POST /api/context/preview` to update three tabs: `Claude Code output`, `Pi output`, and `Full injected prompt`. Add dirty-state tracking, `Save` (PUT only), `Save & Sync` (PUT then POST sync), toast success/error messages, disabled states while requests run, and a visible validation/error area for malformed harness blocks returned by the API. Ensure preview remains read-only and never writes.

subItems.title: Editing a `{{#harness:claude}}` or `{{#harness:pi}}` block updates the appropriate preview tab after debounce without saving to disk.
`Save` writes the selected layer through `PUT /api/context/layers`; `Save & Sync` writes first and then calls `POST /api/context/sync` exactly once.
The Full injected prompt preview includes rendered layer content and explicit memory/status/briefing sections or placeholders for auditability.
Errors from save, preview, or sync are visible to the user and do not discard unsaved editor content.

items.title: PAN-1201: Integrate Context page into dashboard navigation

narrative.Action: Wire the new page into dashboard navigation. Update `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Header.tsx` `Tab` union with `context`; add `/context` to `TAB_PATHS`/`PATH_TO_TAB` and an `activeTab === 'context'` render branch in `src/dashboard/frontend/src/App.tsx`; import the page; add a System nav item in `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Sidebar.tsx` with an appropriate lucide icon; add a CommandPalette navigation action if built-in navigation actions list tabs explicitly. Update `src/dashboard/frontend/src/App.test.tsx`, Sidebar tests if present, and route-normalization tests so `/context` loads the page.

subItems.title: Navigating to `/context` selects the Context tab and renders ContextPage.
The sidebar exposes a Context navigation item under System without removing Skills, Settings, or God View.
Command palette or route tests that enumerate tabs include Context and continue passing for existing tabs.

items.title: PAN-1201: Cover Context dashboard behavior with tests

narrative.Action: Add focused automated tests for the new dashboard Context feature. Server tests should exercise path allowlisting, preview rendering for Claude/Pi blocks, preview read-only behavior, and save writes to the expected temp files. Frontend tests under `src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/context/__tests__/ContextPage.test.tsx` should mock the API/editor and verify layer switching, preview tab updates, dirty state, Save, Save & Sync sequencing, and error handling. Navigation tests should assert `/context` and sidebar access. Keep tests deterministic; if any debounced timers are used in frontend tests, use `vi.useFakeTimers()` and `vi.advanceTimersByTimeAsync()` rather than real timeouts.

subItems.title: Server tests prove preview requests do not write files and save requests cannot escape the context layer allowlist.
Frontend tests cover all three preview tabs, layer selection, dirty state, Save, Save & Sync, and error preservation of unsaved content.
Tests that involve debounce or retry timing use Vitest fake timers per project rules.

items.title: PAN-1201: Update context docs and regression guardrails

narrative.Action: Update `docs/CONTEXT-LAYERS.md` and any relevant skill/help inventory text to describe the dashboard Context page, the plural `pan projects` command, the current `.pan/context` project/workspace paths, the Save vs Save & Sync behavior, and the Full injected prompt pane's scope. Preserve existing `sync-sources/` and layered CLI documentation. If `docs/SKILLS-INVENTORY.md` or pan-sync skill text still says devroot is the source, update it to layered context terminology.

subItems.title: Docs mention `pan projects add <path>` as the project registration command and describe singular `pan project` only as compatibility if at all.
Docs describe the dashboard Context page, layer selector, per-harness previews, Full injected prompt pane, and Save & Sync semantics.
No updated doc tells users that `sync.devroot` or top-level `skills/`/`rules/` are the current source of truth for PAN-1201 context distribution.

items.title: PAN-1201: Verify CLI, tests, build, and isolated browser UAT

narrative.Action: Run the required quality gates and manual dashboard verification after implementation. Commands: run `bun install` if Monaco dependencies changed; run `npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, `npm test`, and targeted tests for context layers/dashboard if useful during debugging. Build the dashboard with `npm run build` before running it. For UI verification, start the dashboard using the Node 22 built dist path via the existing project workflow (`pan up` or the documented Node 22 command, never Bun), open `/context` in an isolated Playwright browser/profile, edit sample harness blocks, confirm Claude/Pi/full previews update, save a temp fixture layer if the route supports fixture mode or otherwise use a safe temporary PANOPTICON_HOME/project fixture, and confirm Save & Sync feedback appears.

subItems.title: `pan projects add --help` and `pan project add --help` both work, with plural documented as preferred.
`npm run typecheck`, `npm run lint`, and `npm test` pass after implementation.
Browser UAT uses an isolated Playwright browser/profile and verifies `/context` loads, layer switching works, live previews update, and Save/Save & Sync show correct success/error feedback.
Dashboard server is run under Node 22 built dist for verification; no Bun dashboard server is used.
